title: It’s not about Anthropic vs. OpenAI anymore
slides: Who's actually winning the AI race now?
Enter DeepSeek: a Chinese lab with $1.4 billion
It trained a model rivaling GPT-4 for a fraction
Your next enterprise vendor may not be American
Audit your current AI supply chain today
thread_text:
1. Who's actually winning?

DeepSeek trained a GPT-4 rival on a fraction of the cost.

2. The $1.4 billion question

That lab built the model with less budget than U.S. giants burn on compute.

3. Your next vendor shift

Enterprise AI procurement may soon bypass American providers entirely.

4. Audit your supply chain

Map every AI tool your team uses. Chances are, one route leads to China.

5. What runs by you?

Pick one AI tool you use weekly. Trace who owns the underlying model today.
video_script: DeepSeek undercut the AI giants by billions.
They trained a GPT-4 competitor on pocket change.
Your next enterprise AI vendor may not be American.
Map your current AI supply chain this week.
Trace who owns the model you use daily.

Your next enterprise AI vendor may not be American.

instagram.caption:
A Chinese lab called DeepSeek trained a model that rivals GPT-4 on a fraction of the budget US giants spend.  

They did it with $1.4 billion—less than what competitors burn on compute alone.  
Your next enterprise AI vendor might not be American.  

For creators and teams: the AI tools you rely on could have a supply chain running through Beijing.  

Map every AI tool your team uses this week: who owns the underlying model? 🇨🇳💻🔍
